mnemonics.title: SST Partitioning: 'Between Walls Total'
mnemonics.content: Remember SST = SSB + SSW as 'The Total is Between the Walls.' SST (Total) is always split into SSB (Between groups) and SSW (Within groups / Walls of each group). The total variation is explained by what happens between groups and what happens within the walls of each group.

mnemonics.title: Degrees of Freedom: 'k-1 Between, N-k Within'
mnemonics.content: Think 'Groups minus one Between, Observations minus Groups Within.' df_Between = k - 1 (one less than the number of groups) and df_Within = N - k (total observations minus number of groups). They always add up to N - 1.

mnemonics.title: F-statistic: 'Far Between, Weak Within'
mnemonics.content: F = MSB / MSW. If group means are Far apart (large MSB) and variation Within groups is Weak (small MSW), the F-value is large and we reject H0. Think: 'A large F means the Factor matters.' F stands for Factor effect.
